The absolute cross sections of angular
distributions were believed to be reliable to within
+-10% for the seven curves from 15.4 to 18.1 MeV,and
to within +-20% for the three curves at lower energies.
Alpha particles were detected with a cylindrical
ionization chamber employing a Frisch grid.

An 02 gas target was used for the runs at laboratory
angles from 45. to 135.degr. At each angle less than
45.degr. the elastic scattering from carbon plus oxygen
was measured using a Mylar target, that from carbon was
measured separately with a polystyrene target, and the
contribution from oxygen was obtained by subtraction.
the protons were detected with a conventional
NaI(Tl) scintillation counter.
